text: Phishing IR Playbook — Containment: (1) Quarantine email from all mailboxes. (2) Block malicious URLs/domains at proxy. (3) Reset credentials of users who interacted. (4) Scan endpoints for IOC presence.

Phishing IR Playbook — Eradication: (1) Remove all copies of the malicious email. (2) Invalidate active sessions. (3) Analyze email headers for origin tracing. (4) Scan for data exfiltration attempts.
Phishing IR Playbook — Recovery: (1) Restore accounts after identity verification. (2) Enable MFA for affected accounts. (3) Communicate to affected users. (4) File report with CIRT.

Ransomware IR Playbook — Immediate Actions: (1) Isolate ALL affected systems from network NOW. (2) Do NOT shut down — preserve memory for forensics. (3) Identify patient zero. (4) Check backup integrity before restoration.

Ransomware IR Playbook — Eradication: (1) Identify and close the initial access vector. (2) Remove ransomware binaries and persistence. (3) Patch the exploited vulnerability. (4) Rotate all credentials.

Ransomware IR Playbook — Recovery: (1) Restore from offline backups in priority order (critical systems first). (2) Reconnect to network gradually with monitoring. (3) Notify CNIL/ANSSI within 72h if personal data breached.

Malware IR Playbook: (1) Isolate endpoint at network level. (2) Capture memory dump before removal. (3) Analyze malware behavior in sandbox. (4) Extract IOCs and share with TI team. (5) Patch exploited vulnerability.

Data Breach IR Playbook: (1) Identify scope and type of data breached. (2) Contain exfiltration vector. (3) Notify DPO/Legal within 24h. (4) Notify CNIL within 72h if PII involved. (5) Prepare customer communication.

DFIR — Evidence Preservation: Chain of custody must be maintained. Capture memory first (volatile data), then disk image. Use forensic write blockers. Hash all artifacts (SHA-256). Store in tamper-proof environment.
DFIR — Memory Forensics: Tools: Volatility3, WinPMEM. Key artifacts: running processes, network connections, injected code, encryption keys. Memory often reveals C2 configurations and credentials.
DFIR — Log Sources for Investigation: Windows Event Logs (4624/4625/4688/4698), Sysmon, PowerShell Script Block Logging, EDR telemetry, Firewall/Proxy logs, DNS logs, Email gateway logs.

IR Communication Plan: Internal: IT Director, Legal, PR, HR. External: CISA/ANSSI, Law Enforcement (if criminal), Affected customers/partners. Never confirm ransom payment publicly. Use dedicated crisis communication channel.

Post-Incident Review (PIR): Conduct within 2 weeks of incident closure. Agenda: timeline reconstruction, root cause analysis, detection gaps, response efficiency, lessons learned, action items with owners and deadlines.

Tabletop Exercise: Simulate ransomware scenario with stakeholders (CISO, Legal, IT, HR, Communications). Test: detection time, escalation process, decision-making, communication. Frequency: at least annual.
